candel
  • API Reference
  • candel.model
  • View page source

candel.model

Base classes

Base classes for peculiar velocity (PV) forward models.

class candel.model.base_pv.BasePVModel(config_path)[source]

Bases: ModelBase

Base class for all PV models.

class candel.model.base_pv.JointPVModel(submodels, shared_param_names)[source]

Bases: object

A joint probabilistic velocity (PV) model that runs multiple submodels (e.g., TFR models) on independent datasets, while sharing a subset of parameters across all submodels.

PV models

TFR (Tully-Fisher relation) forward model.

class candel.model.model_PV_TFR.TFRModel(config_path)[source]

Bases: BasePVModel

A TFR forward model, distance is numerically marginalized out at each MCMC step instead of being sampled as a latent variable.

Supernova forward model.

class candel.model.model_PV_SN.SNModel(config_path)[source]

Bases: BasePVModel

A SNe forward model: the distance is numerically marginalized at each MCMC step. The Tripp coefficients are sampled. The true (x1, c) are analytically marginalised out using the Gaussian conjugacy of the Tripp relation.

Pantheon+ forward model.

class candel.model.model_PV_PantheonPlus.PantheonPlusModel(config_path)[source]

Bases: BasePVModel

Pantheon+ forward model, the distance is numerically marginalized out at each MCMC step instead of being sampled as a latent variable.

Fundamental Plane (FP) forward model.

class candel.model.model_PV_FP.FPModel(config_path)[source]

Bases: BasePVModel

A FP model where the distance modulus μ is integrated out using a grid, instead of being sampled as a latent variable. The true (log σ, log I) are analytically marginalised out using the Gaussian conjugacy of the FP relation.

H0 models

Cepheid-calibrated H0 (CH0) forward model in JAX.

class candel.model.model_H0_CH0.CH0Model(config_path, data)[source]

Bases: ModelBase

Base class for Cepheid-calibrated H0 models, handling configuration, data loading, and numerical grid setup.

sample_host_distmod()[source]

Sample distance moduli for host galaxies, with a uniform prior in the distance modulus. Includes geometric anchor information for NGC 4258, the LMC, and M31.

log_S_cz(lp_r, Vpec, H0, sigma_v)[source]

Probability of detection term if redshift-truncated.

log_S_SN_mag(lp_r, M_SN, H0)[source]

Probability of detection term if supernova magnitude-truncated.

log_S_SN_mag_cz(lp_r, Vpec, M_SN, H0, sigma_v)[source]

Probability of detection term if supernova magnitude and redshift-truncated.

log_S_Cepheid_mag(lp_r, M_W, b_W, Z_W, H0)[source]

Probability of detection term if Cepheid magnitude-truncated.

log_prior_distance(r, **kwargs)[source]

Log prior on the (physical distance).

sigma_v_from_density(delta, sigma_v_low, sigma_v_high, log_rho_t, k)[source]

Map overdensity to sigma_v through a sigmoid in log density.

LOS interpolation

Linear interpolator for line-of-sight (LOS) data on a uniform radial grid.

class candel.model.interp.LOSInterpolator(los_r, f, r0_decay_scale=5, extrap_constant=0.0)[source]

Bases: object

Vectorized 1D interpolator for line-of-sight (LOS) data on a uniform grid.

The expected shapes are:

  • los_r: array of shape (n_steps,) — must be uniform

  • f: array of shape (n_fields, n_galaxies, n_steps)

The interpolator can be queried with:

  • __call__(r) where r has shape (n_galaxies,), returning (n_fields, n_galaxies).

  • interp_many(r_eval) where r_eval has shape (n_eval,), returning (n_fields, n_galaxies, n_eval).

Extrapolation:
For r > r_max, values follow an exponential decay:

f(r) = C + (A - C) * exp(-(r - r_max) / r0)

where A is the last tabulated value, C = extrap_constant, and r0 = r0_decay_scale.

interp_many(r_eval)[source]

Interpolate at shared radii r_eval of shape (n_eval,).

Returns shape (n_field, n_gal, n_eval).

interp_many_steps_per_galaxy(r_eval)

Interpolate at shared radii r_eval of shape (n_eval,).

Returns shape (n_field, n_gal, n_eval).

Simpson integration

Routines copied from:

https://github.com/adrn/jax-ext/blob/main/jax_ext/integrate/simpson.py

Only minor changes were made to the original code.

candel.model.simpson.tupleset(t, i, value)[source]
candel.model.simpson.simpson_log_weights(x)[source]

Log of composite Simpson’s 1/3 weights for a 1D grid.

For N points (N odd, >= 3), the composite Simpson rule is

∫ f dx ≈ Σ_i w_i f(x_i)

This returns log(w_i). Requires all weights positive (true when adjacent spacing ratios stay below 2).

candel.model.simpson.ln_simpson(ln_y, x, axis=-1)[source]

Integrate y(x) using log values of the function ln_y evaluated at the locations x, and return the log of the integral

Note: x values must be increasing and x and ln_y must have the same length.

Parameters:

  • ln_y (array_like) – Array of log function values to be integrated.

  • x (array_like) – The points at which ln_y is evaluated.

Returns:

The estimated log integral computed with the composite Simpson’s rule.

Return type:

float

Utilities

General utility functions for PV and H0 forward models: configuration, physics, priors, and SH0ES helpers.

candel.model.utils.make_json_safe(obj)[source]
candel.model.utils.config_hash(cfg)[source]
candel.model.utils.predict_cz(zcosmo, Vrad)[source]
candel.model.utils.mvn_logpdf_cholesky(y, mu, L)[source]

Log-pdf of a multivariate normal using Cholesky factor L (lower triangular).

candel.model.utils.smoothclip_nr(nr, tau)[source]

Smooth zero-clipping for the number density.

candel.model.utils.log_prior_r_empirical(r, R, p, n, Rmax_grid, Rmax_truncate=None)[source]
Log of the (empirical) truncated prior:

π(r) ∝ r^p * exp(-(r/R)^n), 0 < r ≤ Rmax

Normalized by Z = [R^(1+p) * γ(a, x)] / n with a = (1+p)/n, x = (Rmax/R)^n

class candel.model.utils.JeffreysPrior(low=0.0, high=1.0, *, validate_args=None)[source]

Bases: Uniform

Wrapper around Uniform that keeps Uniform sampling but overrides log_prob to behave like a Jeffreys prior.

Sometimes this is also called a reference prior, or a scale-invariant prior.

log_prob(value)[source]

Evaluates the log probability density for a batch of samples given by value.

Parameters:

value – A batch of samples from the distribution.

Returns:

an array with shape value.shape[:-self.event_shape]

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

class candel.model.utils.Maxwell(scale=1.0, validate_args=None)[source]

Bases: Distribution

Maxwell–Boltzmann (speed) distribution in 3D.

PDF:

f(x; a) = sqrt(2/pi) * x^2 * exp(-x^2 / (2 a^2)) / a^3, x >= 0, a > 0

where a = sqrt(kT/m) is the scale parameter.

Args:

scale (float or array): positive scale parameter a.

Notes:

This is the chi distribution with k=3 degrees of freedom and scale a.

arg_constraints = {'scale': Positive(lower_bound=0.0)}
support = Nonnegative(lower_bound=0.0)
reparametrized_params = ['scale']
sample(key, sample_shape=())[source]

Returns a sample from the distribution having shape given by sample_shape + batch_shape + event_shape. Note that when sample_shape is non-empty, leading dimensions (of size sample_shape) of the returned sample will be filled with iid draws from the distribution instance.

Parameters:

  • key (jax.random.PRNGKey) – the rng_key key to be used for the distribution.

  • sample_shape (tuple) – the sample shape for the distribution.

Returns:

an array of shape sample_shape + batch_shape + event_shape

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

log_prob(value)[source]

Evaluates the log probability density for a batch of samples given by value.

Parameters:

value – A batch of samples from the distribution.

Returns:

an array with shape value.shape[:-self.event_shape]

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

candel.model.utils.load_priors(config_priors)[source]

Load a dictionary of NumPyro distributions from a TOML file.

candel.model.utils.log_integral_gauss_pdf_times_cdf(mu, sigma, t, w)[source]

Log of ∫ N(x|mu, sigma^2) Φ((t - x)/w) dx. Closed form: Φ((mu - t)/sqrt(sigma^2 + w^2))

candel.model.utils.log_prob_integrand_sel(x, e_x, lim, lim_width)[source]
candel.model.utils.logmeanexp(x, axis=None, denom=None)[source]

Stable log(mean(exp(x))) with optional explicit denominator.

PV-specific sampling utilities: vector sampling, galaxy bias, external velocity, distance priors, and TFR/SN helpers.

candel.model.pv_utils.sample_vector_components_uniform(name, low, high)[source]

Sample a 3D vector by drawing each Cartesian component independently from a uniform distribution over [xmin, xmax].

candel.model.pv_utils.sample_vector_fixed(name, mag_min, mag_max)[source]

Sample a 3D vector with direction ~ isotropic, magnitude ~ Uniform.

candel.model.pv_utils.sample_radialmag_vector(name, nval, low, high)[source]

Sample a vector whose magnitude varies at nval knots but a direction is shared by all knots and sampled isotropically on the sky. The magnitude is sampled ~ Uniform(low, high).

Returns the tuple (mag, rhat), where mag has shape (nval,) and rhat has shape (3,).

candel.model.pv_utils.sample_radial_vector(name, nval, low, high)[source]

Sample a radial vector at nval knots: direction ~ isotropic, magnitude ~ Uniform(low, high). Returns an array of shape (nval, 3).

candel.model.pv_utils.interp_cartesian_vector(rq, rknot, v_knot, method='cubic')[source]

Magnitude via interpax; direction via SLERP; constant extrapolation.

candel.model.pv_utils.rsample(name, dist, shared_params=None)[source]

Retrieve a parameter value: use shared_params[name] when provided (for sharing/conditioning across submodels), otherwise draw from dist.

candel.model.pv_utils.marginalise_2d_latent(prior_std1, prior_std2, rho, prior_mean1, prior_mean2, e2_obs1, e2_obs2, obs1, obs2, f1, f2)[source]

Analytically marginalise 2D Gaussian latent variables.

Given a bivariate Gaussian hyperprior on latent x = (x1, x2) with mean (prior_mean1, prior_mean2) and covariance parameterised by (prior_std1, prior_std2, rho), independent Gaussian observations obs1, obs2 with variances e2_obs1, e2_obs2, and a linear projection f = (f1, f2), returns:

f_dot_mu1 : fᵀ μ₁ (posterior mean projected onto f) fSf : fᵀ Σ₁ f (posterior variance projected onto f) log_ev_obs: ln N(d_obs | μ_h, Σ_h + Σ_o)

candel.model.pv_utils.get_absmag_TFR(eta, a_TFR, b_TFR, c_TFR=0.0)[source]

Tully–Fisher absolute magnitude with optional curvature:

M(eta) = a + b * eta + c * eta^2 for eta > 0,

a + b * eta otherwise.

The quadratic term only applies on the high-width (eta > 0) side to capture potential curvature while keeping the low-width branch linear.

candel.model.pv_utils.gauss_hermite_log_weights(n)[source]

Gauss-Hermite nodes and log-weights for Gaussian-weighted integration.

Returns (nodes, log_weights) such that:

∫ f(x) N(x; mu, sigma) dx ≈ Σ_i exp(log_w_i) * f(mu + sqrt(2)*sigma*x_i)

candel.model.pv_utils.sample_galaxy_bias(priors, galaxy_bias, shared_params=None, **kwargs)[source]

Sample a vector of galaxy bias parameters based on the specified model.

candel.model.pv_utils.lp_galaxy_bias(delta, log_rho, bias_params, galaxy_bias, quadratic_bias_delta0=0.0)[source]

Given the galaxy bias probabibility, given some density and a bias model.

candel.model.pv_utils.compute_Vext_radial(data, r_grid, Vext, which_Vext, **kwargs_Vext)[source]

Compute the line-of-sight projection of the external velocity.

Promote the final output to shape (n_field, n_gal, n_rbins).

candel.model.pv_utils.sample_distance_prior(priors)[source]

Sample hyperparameters describing the empirical distance prior.

candel.model.pv_utils.sumzero_basis(npix)[source]

Return an orthonormal basis (npix x (npix - 1)) for the subspace of vectors with zero sum.

candel.model.pv_utils.sample_Vext(priors, which_Vext, shared_params=None, kwargs_Vext={})[source]
candel.model.pv_utils.log_p_S_TFR_eta(eta_mean, w_eta, e_eta, eta_min, eta_max)[source]

Compute the fraction of samples given a truncation in linewidth distribution, whose hyperprior is assumed to be Gaussian.

candel.model.pv_utils.add_sigma_mag_to_lane_cov(sigma_mag, Sigma_d)[source]

Add the intrinsic magnitude scatter to the Lane covariance matrix. It is added along the diagonal and the indices corresponding to the magnitude residuals.
